info.parenttitle: A3.3 Introduction to mechanical systems (HL only)

markdown:
## Mechanical Advantage


<definition term="Mechanical Advantage">The **ratio of output force to input force** in a **mechanical system**.</definition>

<callout type="note">$$\text{Mechanical Advantage (MA)} = \frac{\text{Length of Effort Arm (EA)}}{\text{Length of Resistance Arm (RA)}}$$

* **Mechanical Advantage (MA)** represents the factor by which a lever multiplies the force applied to it 
* **Length of Effort Arm (EA)** is the distance from the fulcrum (the pivot point) to the point where the effort force is applied 
* **Length of Resistance Arm (RA)** is the distance from the fulcrum to the point where the resistance force (Load) is located.</callout>

### Why It Matters


Mechanical advantage helps:

1. **Reduce the effort** needed to move or lift something
2. **Increase output force** for a given input
3. **Improve efficiency** in tasks involving resistance or load  


<callout type="note">* **Mechanical advantage** does not create **energy**, it **redistributes** it. 
* While force is amplified, the distance over which the force is applied is often reduced, maintaining the conservation of energy.</callout>

### Key Simple Systems


#### Lever


<definition term="Lever">A rigid bar that **pivots on a fulcrum** to **move a load with an applied effort**.</definition>

<callout type="note">Lever system breakdown:

* **Fulcrum (pivot)** – The fixed point the lever rotates around.
* **Effort (E)** – The input force you apply to the lever.
* **Load (L)** – The object or resistance being moved.</callout>

![Crowbar lifting a nail (Milwaukee)](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/3f50c3e7f37d5ed1123da7ce311d8637360f1468-1214x1214.png)

#### Inclined Plane


<definition term="Inclined Plane">**Spreads force** over a **longer distance**, making it **easier to lift or move objects**.</definition>

![A ramp to move heavy boxes (willscot)](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/b3141238ed8b9656c67c2714a177bb05c76b9d86-2000x1263.png)

#### Pulley


<definition term="Pulley">A **grooved wheel** that uses a **rope, cord, chain, or cable** to **transmit energy and motion**.</definition>

![Pulley systems are often used on construction sites to move a heavy load](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/fbc8071369926962c018d9545ede17c3cc083f5c-1968x2152.png)

#### Wedge


<definition term="Wedge">A **triangular tool** that **converts force** from its **blunt end** into **forces at right angles** to its **sloped surfaces**.</definition>

![An axe is a wedge that splits wood by redirecting force sideways (the manual)](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/3643d0535510500ca56319868ec5c13b90e6c889-1500x1000.png)

#### Screw


<definition term="Screw">An **inclined plane** wrapped around a **cylinder**, used to **convert rotational force** into **linear motion** or **hold objects together**.</definition>

![A car jack uses a screw mechanism to lift vehicles with minimal effort (Haynes Manuals)](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/5c9b4f422c08cc5f510f5ab757aafbd67eadcbea-3994x1988.png)

### Application


<callout type="example">* **Scissors** combine levers and wedges to cut materials with minimal effort.
* **Assembly lines** incorporate levers and conveyors to move products efficiently, reducing manual labor and increasing productivity.</callout>

<callout type="self_review">Identify three simple machines in your daily life and explain how they provide mechanical advantage. Consider their impact on efficiency and function.</callout>
